## Scorecard

| Category | Score | Rationale |
|----------|-------|-----------|
| Blast radius | Green | Scale-up of replicas; no resource destruction, no data-path reconfiguration. Worst case: new pods fail to schedule (node capacity) — rollback is immediate. |
| Reversibility | Green | `helm rollback notifyapi` or revert the PR restores previous replica count within seconds. |
| Change velocity | Green | No changes to this chart in the past 14 days. |
| Dependency impact | Green | notifyapi has no downstream consumers. Upstream SQS queue and SMTP relay are unaffected by replica count. |
| Timing context | Green | Change scheduled during business hours, no active incident, no traffic freeze in effect. |
| Operator familiarity | Green | Operator has deployed this chart multiple times. Replica scaling is a routine operation. |
| Observability readiness | Green | Deployment rollout is observable via `kubectl rollout status`. Pod readiness probes are configured. |
| Cost impact | Green | 2 additional pods at 250m/256Mi. Estimated monthly delta: ~$8 USD. Within approved budget. |
| Security posture | Green | No IAM, network policy, secret, or image changes. |
| Resource health | Green | Deployment currently healthy (2/2 pods ready). Cluster has sufficient capacity for 4 replicas. |
